repo_prior_art.cite: eid_data_fusion_k_source_v1, Conjecture 1: related overlap-hedge conjecture did not specify a label-uniform two-block context-severing condition.

published_prior_art.cite: ShpitserPearl2006Joint supplies binary hedge countermodels; LeeCorreaBareinboim2020General supplies synchronized gID countermodels, but neither states the selector-context coupling-invisibility condition.

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_data_fusion_k_source_v1, Conjecture 1: same broad SS-ID witness gap, but no terminal cs_fail_2 trace characterization by the proposed CS-hedge.

published_prior_art.cite: LeeGhassamiShpitser2024Systematic provides SS-ID for systematic selection; no verified published result supplies a certificate for the stated terminal failure branch.
ShpitserPearl2006Joint: ordinary R-rooted hedges imply nonidentification for classical interventional queries.

soundness_flags.label: Definition CS-hedge / Theorem 1 / Open-ended Question 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The defining display declares only F_0,F_1 but then uses undefined F_b for b∈{L,R}; it also never defines rho_u on a forest, so the claimed obstruction class and all dependent statements are not mathematically determined.

soundness_flags.label: Proposition 1
soundness_flags.one_line: With L=U=∅, the CS-hedge definition still requires a two-block partition and two target-context bidirected paths, which is strictly stronger than and does not reduce to an ordinary R-rooted hedge.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Open-ended Question 1 / honest_scope
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The completeness question is conditional on finite CS-SCMs and the exact terminal confounded-selector SS-ID trace; it does not assume binary sufficiency, positivity on an open observed-law set, or a polynomial extractor.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Conditional completeness for finite instances in C_cs2 only.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Redefine the certificate with an explicit tuple (F_0,F_L,F_R,F_1), define each induced/restricted forest rho_u(F_b), and state exactly which inclusions make it an ordinary hedge pair.

Replace Proposition 1 by a valid boundary theorem: either prove the CS object specializes to an ordinary hedge after allowing an empty parity block, or state the precise extra two-block subclass it yields.
Add a worked finite labelled ADMG exhibiting the repaired CS-hedge and calculate two compatible CS-SCMs whose every supplied context law agrees while the target differs.

recommended_next_step: Return to Stage -1 to repair the CS-hedge definition and its false ordinary-hedge reduction, then rerun novelty review.
